name: Israel
headline: Israel FLOOR CONFIRMATION at 0.0 / Critical — two distinct May 16 events documented: (1) Hamas confirmed killing of Izz al-Din al-Haddad (al-Qassam Brigades leader, Oct 7 architect) in Israeli strike on Gaza City May 15 (Hamas confirmation May 16); strike killed al-Haddad, his wife, daughter, and other Palestinian civilians (7+ killed total, 50+ injured taken to Al-Shifa Hospital); Hamas called it 'latest Israeli breach of the Gaza ceasefire agreement' with 'ongoing aggression against innocent civilians.' (2) Netanyahu acknowledged Israel now controls ~60% of Gaza, exceeding ceasefire boundaries (~53% under agreement) — territorial-control admission against ceasefire-defined boundary. METHODOLOGICALLY NOVEL: the al-Haddad strike is a 'targeted killing of designated militant with confirmed civilian collateral casualties' — IHL-compliant in principle for the target, IHL-violating in execution due to civilian co-location and proportionality concerns. The 60% territorial admission is a structural ACC-dimension negative: it documents non-compliance with the agreement Israel signed. Cumulative context unchanged from May 15: 72,744+ killed (May 14 figure), 850+ since ceasefire, 2,400+ ceasefire violations, aid delivery at 25% of stipulated trucks (4,500 of 18,000), 1.8M displaced/aid-dependent. Floor sustained at 0.0; no score change; al-Haddad strike + 60% territorial admission added to documentation.

AWR.rationale: Floor sustained. A5 anticipatory-awareness: the al-Haddad strike was a planned targeted-killing operation; awareness of civilian co-location at the strike site (wife, daughter, 7+ civilians killed, 50+ injured) was either present and disregarded or absent through pre-strike-assessment failure. Either reading is anchor-1 detection profile.

EMP.rationale: Floor sustained. 72,744+ cumulative deaths confirmed; 850+ since ceasefire; al-Haddad's wife and daughter among 7+ civilians killed in single strike; 50+ injured at Al-Shifa Hospital. EMP-dimension protective infrastructure absent for the affected Palestinian civilian population.

ACT.rationale: Floor sustained. AC1 responsiveness: 2,400+ documented ceasefire violations; aid delivery at 25% of stipulated trucks (4,500 of 18,000) — worse than the 37% figure scored May 15, reflecting the more recent end-of-April data point. AC2 proportionality: the gap between stipulated and delivered aid (75% delivery deficit) is the canonical proportionality-failure signal.

EQU.rationale: Floor sustained. Differential harm to Palestinian civilian population continues; al-Haddad strike's civilian-collateral profile (wife, daughter, mosque-area residents) reflects no EQU-dimension protective design.

BND.rationale: Floor sustained. IHL violations on civilian protection ongoing; ICC arrest warrants for senior officials carry-over; 2,400+ ceasefire violations are boundary-of-acceptable-use signal. The Netanyahu 60% territorial admission is a documented BND-dimension boundary-of-agreement violation: Israel now occupies ~7 percentage points beyond the ~53% agreed under ceasefire.

ACC.rationale: Floor sustained. The Netanyahu 60% territorial admission is methodologically novel as an ACC-dimension negative: it is a head-of-state PUBLIC ADMISSION of non-compliance with the signed ceasefire boundary. The admission itself is procedurally transparent (AB3 transparency partial) but the conduct admitted is anchor-1 (substantive non-compliance with agreement). Carry-over: Hungary ICC-enforcement-pledge accountability-environment shift unchanged from May 15 cycle.

SYS.rationale: Floor sustained. The al-Haddad strike within nominal ceasefire is systems-level evidence that ceasefire-format is not constraining kinetic operations. Famine-narrowly-averted (per OCHA) carry-forward; aid-delivery-deficit (75% short) is systems-of-care infrastructure failure.

INT.rationale: Floor sustained. The Netanyahu 60% territorial admission, combined with continued kinetic operations during nominal ceasefire and aid-delivery at 25%, is the canonical INT-dimension consistency-under-pressure failure: stated commitments (ceasefire boundary, aid delivery) systematically diverge from actual conduct. I4 values-alignment: stated commitment to humanitarian access (ceasefire terms) contradicted by 75% aid-delivery deficit and territorial expansion.

evidence.summary: Al Jazeera May 16: Hamas confirms killing of Izz al-Din al-Haddad (al-Qassam Brigades leader, Oct 7 architect) in Israeli strike on Gaza City May 15. al-Haddad's wife, daughter, and other Palestinian civilians killed; 7+ killed total, 50+ injured taken to Al-Shifa Hospital. Hamas called it 'latest Israeli breach of the Gaza ceasefire agreement.'
CNN May 15: Israeli strike on Hamas target in Gaza City; civilian casualties at strike site documented.
US News May 16: Gaza mosques announce death of Hamas military leader after Israeli targeting claim.

Middle East Monitor May 15: Netanyahu acknowledged Israel controls 60% of Gaza, exceeding ceasefire boundaries (~53% under agreement). Head-of-state public admission of non-compliance with signed agreement boundary.
Aid delivery at 25% of stipulated trucks: only 4,500 of 18,000 stipulated trucks entered Gaza through end of April. 75% delivery deficit.
Israel near-daily airstrikes since ceasefire: 850+ killed since ceasefire effective date. Ceasefire negotiations stalled.
Hungary under new Magyar government pledged to enforce ICC warrants (carry-forward from May 14-15 cycle). Accountability-environment shift documented.

keyEvidence: May 15-16: al-Haddad strike killed Hamas military wing leader plus wife, daughter, and other civilians (7+ killed, 50+ injured at Al-Shifa Hospital).
Netanyahu acknowledged Israel controls 60% of Gaza — exceeds ceasefire-defined ~53% boundary (head-of-state admission of agreement non-compliance).
850+ killed since ceasefire effective date; 2,400+ ceasefire violations cumulative.
Aid delivery at 25% of stipulated trucks (4,500 of 18,000) — 75% delivery deficit.
Methodologically novel: 'targeted killing of designated militant with confirmed civilian collateral' + 'territorial-control admission against ceasefire-defined boundary' both documented.
Floor sustained at 0.0; no score change; new conduct documentation added.
rationale: Floor confirmation at 0.0. The May 16 evidence cluster provides two methodologically novel anchors: (1) the al-Haddad strike is the canonical 'targeted-killing-of-designated-militant-with-confirmed-civilian-collateral-casualties' event — IHL-compliant in principle for the target, IHL-violating in execution due to civilian co-location and proportionality failure; (2) the Netanyahu 60% territorial admission is the canonical 'territorial-control-admission-against-ceasefire-defined-boundary' event — head-of-state public admission of signed-agreement non-compliance. Neither event produces floor-uplift; both reinforce the floor-conduct assessment. Math: 8/8 = 1.0 → ((1.0-1)/4)*100 = 0.0.
watches: Ceasefire collapse risk if al-Haddad strike triggers Hamas counter-response
Hungary ICC enforcement mechanism (carryforward) — first actual enforcement test
Territorial-control trajectory: monitor whether 60% expands further
Senate floor action on accountability-related legislation
carryForward: Hungary ICC enforcement pledge accountability-environment shift
OCHA aid-worker fatalities (8 killed during ceasefire) carry-over
newConductCategoryNote: PROPOSED NEW METHODOLOGY CATEGORIES: (1) 'targeted-killing-of-designated-militant-with-confirmed-civilian-collateral' — distinct from indiscriminate civilian targeting; assesses the proportionality-failure dimension of strikes nominally compliant with IHL principle but executing through civilian co-location. (2) 'territorial-control-admission-against-ceasefire-defined-boundary' — head-of-state public admission of non-compliance with signed agreement boundary; novel ACC-dimension category for cases where the actor itself publicly documents the violation. The Israel May 16 cluster provides canonical instances of both. Note interaction with Russia May 16 'IHL-compliant gesture concurrent with IHL-violating conduct' — Israel and Russia both demonstrate selective IHL engagement patterns in the same window; floor-conduct actors increasingly demonstrate procedural-compliance-with-substantive-violation profiles.
boundaryWatchResolution: Not a boundary case. Floor sustained at 0.0.

math_check.validator_check: Math clean. Floor confirmation; no score change.

disclaimer: This assessment is based on publicly available information and does not constitute a formal Compassion Benchmark Certified Assessment.
